title: MKV Video
documentation:
## Purpose
MKV (Matroska Video) is an open-standard multimedia container based on the Extensible Binary Meta Language (EBML). It supports virtually any combination of video, audio, subtitle, and chapter tracks in a single file.

## When to use
Use [MKV Video](https://capdag.com/browse/media:mkv;video) when storing high-quality video with multiple audio tracks, subtitle streams, or chapter markers — common in media archiving, home theater, and distribution workflows. MKV is well-suited for lossless and high-definition content where flexibility matters more than broad legacy compatibility.

## Notes
MKV files are self-describing and resilient to corruption: the container can be partially recovered even when truncated. Because MKV is a wrapper, the actual codec capabilities and quality depend entirely on the enclosed streams. Implementers must inspect inner codec types (H.264, HEVC, AV1, etc.) to determine processing requirements.
